edge_retention.description: How long the blade holds a usable edge under a standardized cut-test protocol.
edge_retention.methodology: 200 slices of 1-inch sisal rope, measured against reference BESS score drift.

steel_quality.description: Composite of carbon content, hardness (HRC), grain structure, and heat treatment quality.
steel_quality.methodology: Material spec + Rockwell test + microscopy where available.

balance.description: Weight distribution and pinch-point feel during extended use.

handle_ergonomics.description: Comfort and control over a fatigue protocol.
handle_ergonomics.methodology: 45-minute continuous mince + rock-chop session, graded against blister/fatigue checklist.

out_of_box_sharpness.description: Factory edge quality before any honing.
out_of_box_sharpness.methodology: Single-trial BESS test on unopened unit.

fit_and_finish.description: Quality of grinds, handle-to-bolster transitions, spine rounding, overall build.

corrosion_resistance.description: Resistance to patina/rust under realistic home-kitchen humidity.
corrosion_resistance.methodology: 14-day humidity cabinet exposure after controlled acidic-food contact.

products.name: Nakamura Hamono Aogami #2 Gyuto 210mm
products.description: Third-generation bladesmith forge. Single-smith production, ~18 knives per month.

products.name: Takamura R2 Migaki Gyuto 210mm
products.description: Known for extreme thinness and laser-like geometry.

products.name: Yoshikane SKD Nashiji Gyuto 210mm
products.description: Workshop forge, semi-stainless with high edge retention.

products.name: Konosuke Fujiyama White #2 Gyuto 210mm
products.description: Sakai-forged, Tosa-sharpened. Small-batch, long lead times.

products.name: Mazaki Kurouchi White #2 Gyuto 210mm
products.description: Reactive carbon, known for aggressive geometry.

products.name: Wusthof Classic 8-inch Chef's Knife
products.description: Market standard full-tang forged German knife. Ubiquitous.

products.name: Henckels Professional S 8-inch Chef
products.description: Direct Wusthof competitor. Heavy, durable, soft steel.

products.name: Victorinox Fibrox Pro 8-inch Chef
products.description: Budget workhorse. Stamped blade, very soft steel, legendary reputation for value.

products.name: Global G-2 Classic 8-inch Chef
products.description: Stainless-handled, dimpled grip. Cult-ish status despite soft steel.

products.name: Shun Classic 8-inch Chef
products.description: Kai Corp flagship. Damascus cladding is cosmetic; core is VG-MAX.

products.name: Misono UX10 Gyuto 210mm
products.description: Long-standing professional chef favorite. Stainless, forgiving.

products.name: Tojiro DP VG10 Gyuto 210mm
products.description: Entry-level Japanese gyuto, widely recommended as first serious knife.

products.name: Misen Chef's Knife 8-inch
products.description: Kickstarter DTC. Heavy marketing, hybrid profile.

products.name: Made In Chef Knife 8-inch
products.description: DTC, French-forged. Marketing-heavy, steel is standard.

products.name: Material The Almost 7.5 Knife
products.description: Design-forward DTC. Short length is polarizing.

products.name: MAC MTH-80 Professional 8-inch
products.description: NYT Wirecutter perennial pick. Thin, nimble, stainless.

products.name: Yoshiaki Fujiwara Denka Gyuto 240mm
products.description: Waitlist-only. Notorious edge retention, heavy for gyuto.

products.name: Masamoto KS Gyuto 210mm
products.description: Tokyo institution. Favored by professional Japanese kitchens.

attestations.methodology_notes: Aogami #2 at 63 HRC responds exactly as expected. Edge drifted from BESS 120 to 180 over 200 rope cuts.
Best-in-class heat treatment. Wa handle transition is clean.
Carbon patinates fast — expected. Edge holds through tomatoes-to-squash workload.
Geometry is thin behind the edge without being fragile.
42-day long-haul test. Only needed stropping twice.
Microscopy confirms fine grain. Proper quench.
Service-kitchen pace for two weeks. Held up.
Broke down a shoulder. Edge survived bone contact (user error).
French prep workload. Excellent on vegetable brunoise.
Wa handle was unfamiliar but adjusted in a week.
Coming from German knives, geometry was a revelation.
R2 powdered steel at 63 HRC. Thin geometry feels fragile on hard squash.
Good service knife if you respect the edge.
R2 holds long. Chips if you abuse it on bone.
Very thin; be careful with hard vegetables.
SKD tool steel semi-stainless. Sweet spot.
Holds edge longer than Aogami in this sample.
Tool steel with excellent heat treat.
Workhorse. Near-zero maintenance vs full carbon.
Shirogami #2 done right. Light in hand.
Competes with Nakamura on finish, not on edge retention.
Elegant. Not a first carbon knife.
Very reactive, aggressive convex. Purist pick.
Too reactive for most home cooks.
X50CrMoV15 at 58 HRC is what it is. Dulls within a week of daily use.
Reliable home knife. Honing maintains usable edge.
You can drop it and it's fine.
Benchmark German. Nothing more, nothing less.
Near-clone of Wusthof, slightly softer steel.
Cheap Henckels are different — this Pro S is solid.
Stamped, soft, sharp-enough out of box. Needs frequent honing.
Pro-kitchen disposable. Great for the price.
Light; love it or hate it.
VG-MAX core is respectable; Damascus is cosmetic.
Chips if used on hard roots at this geometry.
Professional favorite. Swedish stainless is balanced.
If you want stainless and a western handle, this.
AUS-10 at this price is fine.
Feels like Wusthof at DTC pricing.
Thin, nimble, ubiquitous recommendation for good reason.
Aogami Super at 65 HRC. Unicorn tier. Heavy 240.
Highest edge retention in any test I've run.
